body: PT Bali Media Telekomunikasi (BMT) has increased its stake in PT Smartfren Telecom Tbk (FREN) to 41.17% by acquiring 22.85 billion shares worth IDR 571.40 billion, but FREN's stock performance has not improved since the transaction. FREN's shares have stagnated at IDR 25 per share, down 50% from IDR 50 per share at the beginning of the year, with controlling parties holding 68.12% of shares and non-controllers holding 31.79%.
title: PT Bali Media Telekomunikasi increases stake in PT Smartfren Telecom to 41.17% but fails to boost stock performance.

body: The Indonesian government has set a tobacco excise target of RP230.09 trillion for 2025, a 6.5% reduction from this year's target, and has no plans to increase tobacco excise rates in 2025. Following this news, shares of major tobacco companies such as PT Hanjaya Mandala Sampoerna Tbk (HMSP), PT Gudang Garam Tbk (GGRM), PT Wismilak Inti Makmur Tbk (WIIM), and PT Indonesian Tobacco Tbk (ITIC) saw gains, with HMSP rising 2% to RP665 per share, GGRM up 2% to IDR13,675 per share, WIIM increasing 1% to RP765 per share, and ITIC rising 2% to RP266 per share.
title: Indonesian government sets lower tobacco excise target for 2025, sparking gains in major tobacco company shares.

body: PT Global Digital Niaga Tbk (BLLI) will issue 3.18 billion shares as part of its Management Employee Stock Option Plan (MESOP) program, with a potential value of IDR 1.36 trillion (approximately USD 90 million) at an implementation price of IDR 430. The MESOP program will be implemented in stages, with Phase III offering up to 2.77 billion shares, and Phases II and I offering up to 145.15 million and 400 million shares, respectively.
title: Blibli.com to Issue 3.18 Billion Shares Worth IDR 1.36 Trillion Through Employee Stock Option Plan.

body: The Jakarta Composite Stock Price Index (JCI) corrected 0.45% to 7,293.96 in today's first trading session, with a transaction value of IDR 5.1 trillion and 268 shares showing an uptrend. The financial sector weighed on the JCI, with PT Bank Mandiri Tbk (BMRI) shares being the top laggard, contributing to a 15.7 index point decline, followed by PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia Tbk (BBRI) and PT Telkom Indonesia Tbk (TLKM).
title: Jakarta Composite Index Falls 0.45% in First Trading Session Today

body: PT Sigma Energy Compressindo TBK (SICO) expects a 12% revenue increase in 2025, targeting IDR 119.81 billion, driven by strategies such as improving compressor fleet utilization and exploring new business opportunities. The company's shares have also performed well, jumping 9% since the beginning of the year, with a current share price of IDR 108, up from IDR 100 earlier this year.
title: PT Sigma Energy Compressindo targets 12% revenue growth in 2025 driven by strategic initiatives.

body: Foreign investors continued their net purchase trend in Jakarta, with a total net foreign buy worth IDR 744.64 billion yesterday, following a net foreign buy of IDR 2.08 trillion (USD 130.42 million) on Tuesday. The top 5 stocks with the highest foreign net buy volumes came from various sectors, led by PT GoTo Gojek Tokopedia Tbk (GOTO) with 319.86 million shares, followed by PT Astrindo Nusantara Infrastruktur Tbk, PT Darma Henwa Tbk, PT Bumi Resources Minerals Tbk, and PT J Resources Asia Pasifik Tbk.
title: Foreign investors continue to buy Indonesian stocks, with net purchases reaching IDR 744.64 billion yesterday.

body: Indonesian President Prabowo Subianto stated that the country's stock market is unfriendly to retail investors, who will likely lose to large-scale players, and warned that his "Makan Bergizi Gratis" program could lead to a substantial fiscal burden. Morgan Stanley (MS) lowered its investment rating for Indonesia's stock exchange, citing concerns over the program's potential impact on the country's state income and fiscal burden.
title: President Prabowo Subianto warns that Indonesian stock market favors large-scale players, making it challenging for retail investors to succeed.

body: The Jakarta Composite Stock Price Index (JCI) rose 1.82% or 130.75 points to 7,326.76, marking a three-day upward rally, with top gainers including PT Barito Renewables Energy Tbk (up 9.93%), PT Chandra Asri Pacific Tbk (up 8.60%), and PT Barito Pacific Tbk (up 6.94%). Foreign investors recorded a net purchase of IDR 744.64 billion (USD 46.67 million), with banking issuers such as PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia Tbk, PT Bank Mandiri Tbk, and PT Bank Negara Indonesia Tbk also among the top gainers.
title: Jakarta Composite Stock Price Index Rises 1.82% to 7,326.76 on Wednesday

body: Foreign investors purchased a net volume of 572.64 million shares, with notable buys including 319.86 million shares of PT GoTo Gojek Tokopedia Tbk (GOTO) and 199.90 million shares of PT Astrindo Nusantara Infrastruktur Tbk. Meanwhile, foreign investors sold a significant number of shares in companies such as PT Bumi Resources Tbk (BUMI), PT Bukalapak.com Tbk (BUKA), and PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia Tbk (BBRI), with net volumes ranging from -28.97 million to -136.70 million shares.
title: Foreign investors purchase 4.37 billion shares in Indonesian companies, led by digital firm PT GoTo Gojek Tokopedia Tbk.

body: PT Dayamitra Telekomunikasi Tbk (MTel) has acquired PT Ultra Mandiri Telekomunikasi (UMT) and its subsidiary PT PP Infrastruktur for IDR 650 billion, expanding its fibre optic portfolio and reinforcing its business ecosystem. The acquisition is expected to help MTel become the largest digital infrastructure partner in Asia Pacific, consolidating its tower, fibre optic, and other supporting services.
title: Mtel Acquires PT Ultra Mandiri Telekomunikasi to Strengthen Fibre Optic Portfolio and Reinforce Market Share.

body: PT Medco Energi Internasional Tbk (MEDC) will issue a shelf-registration bond worth IDR 2.5 trillion, consisting of two series with fixed yields of 7.75% and 8.25% per annum, to refinance its maturing bonds and loans, including Series B Shelf-Registration Bond III Phase III Year 2020 and Loan Principal Medco Bell Pte Ltd. The joint underwriters, including PT BRI Danareksa Sekuritas, PT DBS Vickers Sekuritas Indonesia, PT Mandiri Sekuritas, and PT Trimegah Sekuritas Indonesia Tbk, will each account for 25% of the total value, with PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia Tbk appointed as trustee.
title: Medco Energi to Issue IDR 2.5 Trillion Bonds to Refinance Maturing Debt

body: Individual investors, including Manoj Pitamber Nanwani and Johnson Williang Sutjipto, acquired shares in PT Wintermar Offshore Marine Tbk (WINS), increasing their combined stake to around 7% of the company, which provides transportation services to the oil and gas sector. Meanwhile, PT Samuel Tumbuh Bersama made the largest share purchase, acquiring 2.57 billion shares of PT Nusantara Sawit Sejahtera Tbk, increasing its ownership to nearly 50%, while other investors made notable purchases and sales in various companies, including PT Samcro Hyosung Adilestari Tbk, PT Total Bangun Persada Tbk, and PT Astrindo Nusantara Infrastruktur Tbk.
title: Individual investors and foreign firms actively trade shares in various Indonesian companies across different sectors.

body: PT Maybank Sekuritas Indonesia acquired 8.6% of PT Alam Sutera Realty Tbk's shares, while PT Manunggal Prime Development reduced its stake from 26.81% to 21.08%. Other notable transactions include PT Jalan Terang Samudra's 23.19% stake in PT Logindo Samudramakmur Tbk, PT Bhinneka Life Indonesia's 10.44% stake in PT Surya Permata Andalan Tbk, and Eastern Peak Group Corporation's 5.64% stake in PT Bhakti Multi Artha Tbk.
title: Multiple investors make significant changes to their stakes in various Indonesian companies, including real estate, oil and gas, and financial services firms.

body: Foreign investors purchased a net IDR 1.26 trillion worth of PT MD Entertainment Tbk (FILM) shares, acquiring 484.99 million shares, which led to a 5.81% increase in the company's share price to IDR 3,460 per share. The company's management had recently requested approval for a rights issue of 989.77 million shares, with PT MD Global Investments controlling 49.38% of the company, followed by Morgan Stanley Co International with 14.3%.
title: Foreign investors purchase 484.99 million shares of PT MD Entertainment Tbk worth IDR 1.26 trillion.

body: PT Barito Renewables Energy Tbk (BREN) saw its stock price surge IDR 300 to IDR 6,825 per share after announcing an interim dividend distribution of IDR 506.15 billion or IDR 3.78 per share, driven by its improved Q3 2024 performance with net profit of USD 86.05 million and total equity of USD 376 million. The company's financial metrics have improved significantly compared to Q3 2023, with net profit up from USD 84.47 million and total equity up from USD 444.10 million.
title: PT Barito Renewables Energy's stock price surges after announcing interim dividend distribution of IDR 506.15 billion.
